article_meta.title: Politics Why Minnesotans Still Haven't Backed Down From Fighting ICE It's freezing

reasoning: The summary largely avoids Slate’s highly charged rhetoric and offers a more neutral paraphrase, though it introduces a few mildly loaded phrases (e.g., “high-stakes struggle,” “protect undocumented neighbors,” “sheltering ICE agents”). The main deficiency is in sources_represented, which lists only “Slate” and omits the activist/resident perspective explicitly present.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses a few interpretive terms (“protect,” “high-stakes struggle,” “sheltering”) but mostly neutral verbs.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims align with article content (protests, tactics, risks) and avoid asserting disputed outcomes.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Some evaluative framing (“high-stakes struggle,” community motives) is presented in the model’s voice, though grounded in article themes.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tone is restrained compared to the source; only mildly dramatic phrasing appears.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Infers motivations of residents (“rooted in necessity/solidarity”) without explicit attribution, but consistent with quotes.
custom_reasoning.slant: Tracks the article’s one-sided perspective without overemphasis beyond the source.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Summary includes key elements (tactics, risks, protests), with minimal omission.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: “Undocumented neighbors” and “sheltering ICE agents” have mild ideological charge.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: No undue amplification of negative content beyond source.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege elites; mirrors the article’s grassroots focus.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Significantly more neutral than the source’s charged language.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: sources_represented lists only “Slate,” omitting the activists/residents perspective explicitly present in the article.
